news.title:
spark strategy lead: spark delisted rsETH in january, unaffected by recent security incident  

spar
news.summary:
spark strategy lead: spark delisted rsETH in january, unaffected by recent security incident  

spark delisted rsETH and other low-utilization assets in january and has continued to tighten its collateral scope and feature set. although this decision was unpopular among ETH looping users and likely caused spark to lose significant business and revenue to aave. however, sparklend still maintains ample ETH withdrawal liquidity under current conditions, while aave is already experiencing liquidity shortages in markets across multiple networks. nitter.net/monetsupply/status/204…

news.title:
layerzero: kelpdao loses ~$290m in exploit, attributed to dprk’s lazarus group

layerzero reported t
news.summary:
layerzero: kelpdao loses ~$290m in exploit, attributed to dprk’s lazarus group

layerzero reported that on april 18, 2026, kelpdao suffered an exploit resulting in losses of approximately $290m, preliminarily attributed to dprk’s lazarus group (tradertraitor). the attack poisoned downstream rpcs used by the dvn and, combined with ddos, forced failover, causing the dvn to validate transactions that never occurred, without exploiting protocol or key vulnerabilities. the incident stemmed from kelpdao’s 1/1 dvn setup, creating a single point of failure; impact was limited to rsETH with no broader contagion. affected rpcs have been replaced and the layerzero labs dvn is now operational.
nitter.net/layerzero_core/status/…

news.summary:
last week 비트코인 spot etfs logged $996m inflow, extending streak to 3 weeks

from april 13 to april 17 (et), spot 비트코인 etfs recorded net inflows of $996 million, marking the third consecutive week of net inflows. spot 이더리움 etfs saw net inflows of $276 million. SOL spot etfs recorded net inflows of $35.17 million, while XRP spot etfs had net inflows of $55.39 million.

sulaiman ahmed (@shaykhsulaiman)

ceasefire update: iran-u.s draft proposal

✅ iran agrees
news.summary:
협상 초안이래


sulaiman ahmed (@shaykhsulaiman)

ceasefire update: iran-u.s draft proposal

✅ iran agrees to a 15-year enrichment suspension, with the exception of research reactors for medical isotopes.

✅ iran’s stockpile of 60% and 20% uranium would be partially converted to reactor fuel, and partially ‘downblended’ to a lower enrichment level. however, it won’t leave iran.

✅ full iaea supervison on iran’s nuclear sites.

✅ opening the strait of hormuz, with the possibility of collecting tolls.

✅ the u.s. will offer iran phased 제재 relief

✅ the u.s. will unfreeze all iranian assets currently frozen abroad, amounting to $20 billion or more.

✅ the u.s. will commit to a non-aggression pact with iran that is ensured through a un national security council reSOLution and a treaty ratified by congress.

✅ the u.s. will withdraw all its military forces from the persian gulf.

source: amit segal israel channel 12







— https://nitter.net/shaykhsulaiman/status/2045943074040197137#m

news.title: 🇦🇪🇺🇸 the wall street journal flagged a uae request for a 연준 swap line and suddenly the timeline lit
news.summary:
🇦🇪🇺🇸 the wall street journal flagged a uae request for a 연준 swap line and suddenly the timeline lit up: “they’re running out of dollars,” “oil in yuan is next,” “dollar doom.”

none of that holds up.

start with reality: the uae isn’t some cash-strapped economy scrambling for liquidity. it’s sitting on one of the largest sovereign wealth stacks on the planet and is deeply loaded with u.s. assets, especially treasuries.

that matters, because a swap line isn’t a bailout, it’s a tool.

here’s what’s actually happening:

when global dollar demand spikes, countries typically raise cash by selling assets… like u.s. treasuries.

the uae is saying: instead of 하락ing treasuries and pressuring bond markets, let us post them as collateral along with dirhams and access dollars directly through the 연준.

it’s the same plumbing the 연준 has used with the bank of japan, the ecb, and others during liquidity crunches. the difference? the uae wants access without waiting for a crisis.

and the yuan narrative? that simply doesn’t add up.

if you need dollars, selling oil in yuan doesn’t SOLve your problem… it adds a conversion step and fx risk. you’d still need dollars at the end of the chain.

so no, this isn’t a pivot away from the dollar, it’s a major dollar holder asking for a cleaner, more direct line into the dollar system so it doesn’t have to destabilize it by selling treasuries or dirhams.




mario nawfal (@marionawfal)

fmr advisor to uae president just recommended the removal of american bases from the country!

"the uae no longer needs america to defend it. what the uae needs is to acquire only the best and latest weapons that america has. therefore, it is time to think about closing the american bases, as they are a burden and not a strategic asset."

i've always said the repercussions of this war will be felt for generations.

this is exactly what i meant!

source: @abdulkhaleq_uae





— https://nitter.net/marionawfal/status/2045959514378076475#m

news.title:
the us wage growth gap is historically wide:

after-tax wages for higher-income households rose +5.6
news.summary:
the us wage growth gap is historically wide:

after-tax wages for higher-income households rose +5.6% yoy in march on a 3-month moving average basis, the highest in at least 3 years.

at the same time, lower-income earners saw their wages grow just+1.0% yoy, near the lowest in at least 3 years.

this marks the widest gap between the highest and lowest earners since data began in 2015.

by comparison, middle-income wages grew +2.0% yoy last month, near the lowest since mid-2024.

meanwhile, us cpi 인플레이션 jumped +3.3% yoy in march, meaning lower and middle-income households are seeing their real wages decline.

in other words, 인플레이션 is eating all of the wage gains for the bottom two-thirds of american households.

asset owners are the only winners.

news.title:
the global smartphone market is facing a historic decline:

global smartphone shipments are set to f
news.summary:
the global smartphone market is facing a historic decline:

global smartphone shipments are set to fall -13% yoy in 2026, or -160 million units, to ~1.1 billion, according to idc.

this marks a sharp drop from a +2% growth in 2025 and +6% in 2024.

the decline is being driven by an unprecedented memory chip shortage that is inflating component costs across the industry.

as a result, smartphone makers are discontinuing unprofitable entry-level models and pushing consumers toward higher-priced devices.

last year, ~170 million smartphones shipped for under $100, a segment currently uneconomical to maintain.

the shortage is expected to persist into mid-2027, and even when supply returns, memory prices are unlikely to fall back to 2025 levels.

the days of cheap smartphones are over.

news.title:
market liquidity is improving:

top of book depth in the S&P 500 futures jumped to $10.8 million in
news.summary:
market liquidity is improving:

top of book depth in the S&P 500 futures jumped to $10.8 million in the week ending tuesday, the highest since january.

this marks a +440% increase from the ~$2.0 million low recorded last month.

the current reading is also higher than 84.6% of all observations over the last 2 years.

by comparison, the peak over this period was ~$17.0 million, recorded during the market's 랠리 following president 트럼프's 2024 election victory.

etfs accounted for ~28% of total volume in the week ending tuesday, in-line with the historical average, after exceeding 40% for most of march.

falling etf trading volumes are typically associated with calmer markets and greater investor confidence in individual stock selection.

market conditions are stabilizing.

news.title:
retail investors are leading the historic 랠리:

retail's favorite stocks are outperforming mutual fun
news.summary:
retail investors are leading the historic 랠리:

retail's favorite stocks are outperforming mutual fund favorite names by +11 percentage points so far in april.

this puts retail on track for their best relative month since november 2020 after 5 consecutive months of relative underperformance.

as a result, the basket of stocks most favored by retail investors has rallied +22% since late march.

furthermore, the meme stock etf, $meme, has 급등d +56% since march 31st, on track for its best monthly performance since its inception in october 2025.

bullish retail investor sentiment is surging.

news.title:
ukraine struck 2 large russian landing ships yesterday.

the ropucha-class landing ship yamal ($80 m
news.summary:
ukraine struck 2 large russian landing ships yesterday.

the ropucha-class landing ship yamal ($80 million) and the alligator-class landing ship nikolay filchenkov ($70 million) were both hit in the port of sevastopol. a number of russian sailors were likely killed. nitter.net/front_ukrainian/status…
